Memory Dump Tool (XbMemDump)

The Memory Dump tool (XbMemDump) displays the active memory state of an Xbox console while it is running a game. This display allows developers to see how memory allocations are fragmenting and potentially blocking them from making physical allocations. The tool shows the entire 64 or 128 MB memory configuration (by pages), and designates each page by its usage. In addition, XbMemDump allows memory tracking by allocation type.

USAGE: xbmemdump [options]
Option Description
-s Suppresses summary information at the top of the report. By default, XbMemDump generates a summary of its findings and includes the summary at the top of its report.
-l Displays the page frame number layout in a long output format. In this format, memory is broken up by usage, and the size of the memory for each use is displayed.
-w Displays the page frame number layout in a wide format. In this format, memory is broken up in 4K blocks, each block showing its usage. By default, each line shows 64 of these blocks.
-n Size Used with -w option to specify the number of blocks per line.
-vad Displays the virtual address descriptor. All threads are suspended while XbMemDump downloads information.
-x XboxName Xbox name or Xbox IP. Specify a particular target machine.
-e n Enables permanent allocation tracking for every title until reset by the -d option. The parameter n specifies the maximum level of stack trace to capture (0–32). This option only takes effect after the next console reboot.
-d Disables memory allocation tracking set by the -e option. This option only takes effect after the next console reboot.
-m Displays memory allocation tracking history. Allocation tracking must be enabled (see the -r and -e options); all threads are suspended while downloading history information.
-r xbe n Runs a specific XBE with memory allocation tracking enabled. The parameter n (0–32) specifies the maximum level of stack trace to capture. Specify xbe as the full path and file name of the XBE (for example, xd:\filename.xbe). This option is independent of the -e and -d options.
-p path The path parameter specifies the path containing .pdb symbol files for the XBE.

Memory Allocation Tracking

The XbMemDump tool can track different types of memory allocation, both internal and custom-defined, for your title. You can register your own custom allocation type with the DmRegisterAllocationType function. Once you have a custom allocation type registered, call DmInsertAllocationEntry to add the custom type to the XbMemDump tool's tracking database. When you are finished tracking a custom type, you can remove it from the tracking database with the DmRemoveAllocationEntry function.

If you need to look up the description for a particular memory allocation type, use the DmQueryAllocationTypeName function.

Memory Type Abbreviations

The XbMemDump tool uses single-character abbreviations in its output to designate different types of memory. The following table describes each of these abbreviations:

Character Description
s Stack – Pages marked for use as a stack by any of the running threads on the Xbox console.
# Virtual page table – A section of memory reserved by the CPU and the system code to hold the tables that map physical addresses to virtual addresses.
$ System page table – A section of memory used by the CPU and the system code to hold the tables that identify physical pages, and to subsequently map them into the physical address space.
p Pool – A section of memory reserved by the system for system memory allocations.
V Virtual mapped – Physical pages currently used to store virtual pages.
* System memory – Similar to the pool, this is memory allocated by system-level components.
i Image – Any physical pages marked "executable." This is an XBE image loaded into memory.
c File system cache – Memory reserved by the file system cache to hold file system metadata, such as directory entries and the location of file data.
@ Contiguous memory – Physically contiguous memory, allocated using XPhysicalAlloc or D3D_AllocContiguousMemory. This type of memory is guaranteed to always be in sequential physical pages.
d Debugger – Memory allocated by the XBDM debug manager or by the DmAllocatePool function to allow remote debugging of a title over the network.

See Also

(Remote) Target System Tools | DmInsertAllocationEntry, DmQueryAllocationTypeName, DmRegisterAllocationType, DmRemoveAllocationEntry
